title: The Day I Quit — Ranjan Dasgupta | A Story
description: The day I quit. A personal story from Ranjan Dasgupta.
content:
I didn't quit AdTech. I've been in it for nine-plus years—Google, Glance, InMobi. But there was a day when I quit something: a role, a project, a version of myself. Ranjan Dasgupta doesn't overshare; I'll just say that the best decisions sometimes look like quitting from the outside. See the day I started and burnout and back.

Work, About, Contact.

title: The Day I Started — Ranjan Dasgupta | How It Began
description: The day I started: how Ranjan Dasgupta began in AdTech.
content:
Everyone has a day one. Ranjan Dasgupta started in AdTech at Google—DoubleClick, DFP, AdX, AdSense. That day led to Glance ($200M+ scaled), InMobi (exchange strategy), and AdsGupta. See origin story and the day I quit for more of the arc.

Work, About, Contact.

title: My Worst Campaign — Ranjan Dasgupta | What Went Wrong
description: My worst campaign: what went wrong. A candid take by Ranjan Dasgupta.
content:
I've had launches that flopped. Wrong timing, wrong audience, wrong creative. Ranjan Dasgupta doesn't name names, but the lesson is the same: ship, learn, iterate. See my best campaign, killed campaigns, and idea graveyard.

Work, About, Contact.

title: My Best Campaign — Ranjan Dasgupta | What Went Right
description: My best campaign: what went right. By Ranjan Dasgupta.
content:
The best work isn't always the biggest. Sometimes it's the one that nailed the brief, the one that scaled cleanly, or the one that taught the team something. Ranjan Dasgupta has been part of monetization at Glance ($200M+), exchange strategy at InMobi, and product at Google. The "best" is spread across Work. See my worst campaign for the flip side.

About, Contact.

title: Midnight on a Deadline — Ranjan Dasgupta | The Push
description: Midnight on a deadline: the push. By Ranjan Dasgupta.
content:
Everyone in AdTech has been there: midnight, a deadline, a launch. Ranjan Dasgupta has shipped at that hour—releases, fixes, rollouts. It's not romantic; it's the job. See burnout and back and why I do this for the human side.

Work, About, Contact.

title: What Clients Really Mean — Ranjan Dasgupta | Decoding the Brief
description: What clients really mean: decoding the brief. By Ranjan Dasgupta.
content:
"Make it pop" often means "increase CTR." "We need more visibility" often means "we need more yield." Ranjan Dasgupta has spent years translating between client language and AdTech reality. See my brain is a brief and strategy mode.

Work, Contact.

title: Thanks Mom — Ranjan Dasgupta | A Note of Thanks
description: Thanks Mom: a note of thanks from Ranjan Dasgupta.
content: Not every page on this site is about AdTech. This one is a thank-you. Ranjan Dasgupta wouldn't be here without the people who believed first. For the rest of the site: About, Work, the day an ad changed my life, Contact.

title: The Day an Ad Changed My Life — Ranjan Dasgupta | One Ad
description: The day an ad changed my life: one ad that mattered. By Ranjan Dasgupta.
breadcrumbName: The Day an Ad Changed My Life
h1: The day an ad changed my life
content:
It might have been a campaign I worked on. It might have been one I saw. Ranjan Dasgupta has been in the industry long enough to know that ads can inform, entertain, and sometimes change a path. See why ads matter and thanks Mom.

Work, About, Contact.

title: Why I Do This — Ranjan Dasgupta | The Why
description: Why I do this: the why behind the work. By Ranjan Dasgupta.
content:
I do this because the pipes matter. Programmatic, yield, exchange strategy—they fund a lot of what we read and use. Ranjan Dasgupta has spent nine-plus years making those pipes better: transparent, scalable, publisher-first. See my manifesto and why ads matter.

Work, About, Contact.

title: Burnout and Back — Ranjan Dasgupta | Recovery
description: Burnout and back: recovery and return. By Ranjan Dasgupta.
content:
AdTech is intense. Deadlines, scale, stakes. Ranjan Dasgupta has seen burnout—in himself and in others. Coming back means boundaries, purpose, and sometimes saying no. See the day I quit, midnight on a deadline, and why I do this.

Work, About, Contact.
